title: Scrap pattern detected on Laser #2
summary: 3 jobs affected this week. Hypothesis: nozzle wear.
hypothesis: Cut edge kerf has drifted +0.014" over the last 72 hours on 1/2" A36 plate. Consistent with a nozzle approaching end-of-life (typical replacement interval: ~420 pierce hours; current nozzle at 404 hrs).
evidence: Scrap rate on laser-2 jumped from 1.8% (rolling 30d) to 4.9% (last 7d).
3 of 4 affected parts share the same cut program (PRG-A36-050-STRING-V2).
QC measurements show +0.012" to +0.018" kerf widening across samples.
No change in material vendor or sheet lot during this period.
recommendations: Replace nozzle tip on Laser #2 before next shift (est. downtime: 12 min).
Re-verify PRG-A36-050-STRING-V2 kerf offset after nozzle change.
Place a QC hold on WO-2026-00218 stringer cuts until verified.
